description: Lock the named evidence scopes, resolved dates, and removal of generic ingredient, category, and chronology-breaking dependencies for the second archaeological chronology batch.

checks.reason: The Lascaux lamps are shaped sandstone and limestone rather than ceramic vessels.
Animal-fat fuel does not identify domesticated livestock as its source.
Combustion is the documented operating method of the fat lamps.
The rounded date remains anchored to the official 21,500-21,000 BP Lascaux occupation range.
Managed caprine herds constitute the Wadi Jilat pastoralist claim.
The rounded date remains anchored to Wadi Jilat 13 ranges beginning near 7030 cal BCE.
The scoped pastoralists participated in long-standing networks and postdate the trade-route node.
Shared shelter category does not establish a causal lineage between Ohalo II and Garvald Burn.
The probable windbreak remains anchored to the 4350-4000 cal BCE posthole date.
Animal fat is one possible lipid source and does not make husbandry universal.
Water use in a recipe does not establish a transport-technology dependency.
The date remains at the ACS review's third-millennium BCE empirical boundary.
The scoped Tell es-Sultan installation is surviving stone architecture.
Communal labor does not by itself prove ranked social classes.
The wall and tower are constitutively stone construction.
The PPNA wall-and-tower record remains anchored to roughly 8300 BCE.
Cato's sequence salts and air-dries the ham before smoking.
Cato explicitly requires two days in smoke.
The surviving procedure remains anchored to the approximate composition of On Agriculture.
A specific harvesting implement is not required by the threshing-sledge evidence.
The scoped evidence is a sledge, not basket winnowing.
Storage is downstream of cereal separation rather than its enabler.
Neolithic cereal agriculture supplies the crop-processing system.
Manufactured flaked-stone inserts are the direct archaeological evidence.
Managed cattle provide the animal-traction pathway described for the sledge.
The inferred Greek sledge use remains anchored to about 6500 cal BCE.
The Pseudo-Democritean text is not a duplicate of Blombos pigment processing.
The alchemical corpus remains anchored to its uncertain first- or second-century CE composition.
A first- or second-century CE text cannot precede a 1500 BCE slag-reuse record.
